summary: Streaming aggregation results streams entire collection
insert with {fullResult: true} should return full document
Really poor query performance
description:
Several links to symptoms of the issue. At the end of the day, this looks like a problem in 2.0.x but not 1.4.x -- which is a huge problem since I just switched all my code to 2.0.x. This bug has severe performance issues on queries, reducing performance anywhere from 25x - 100x.

http://stackoverflow.com/questions/27830277/odd-mongodb-node-explain-results

http://stackoverflow.com/questions/27831936/limit-1-1ms-limit-1-150ms-mongo-melt-down

http://stackoverflow.com/questions/27844128/mongodb-native-node-driver-explain-is-broken

summary: Cannot read property 'hasListIndexesCommand' of undefined
description:
I'm intermittently getting the following error in my Node.JS application which depends on the Node.JS MongoDB driver.

{code}
	TypeError: Cannot read property 'hasListIndexesCommand' of undefined
	    at Collection.listIndexes (/path/to/node_modules/connect-mongo/node_modules/mongodb/lib/mongodb/collection/index.js:84:49)
	    at Db.indexInformation (/path/to/node_modules/connect-mongo/node_modules/mongodb/lib/mongodb/db.js:1598:25)
	    at Db.ensureIndex (/path/to/node_modules/connect-mongo/node_modules/mongodb/lib/mongodb/db.js:1465:8)
	    at Collection.ensureIndex (/path/to/node_modules/connect-mongo/node_modules/mongodb/lib/mongodb/collection/index.js:68:11)
	    at module.exports._get_collection (/path/to/node_modules/connect-mongo/lib/connect-mongo.js:167:29)
	    at Db.collection (/path/to/node_modules/connect-mongo/node_modules/mongodb/lib/mongodb/db.js:496:20)
	    at MongoStore.module.exports._get_collection (/path/to/node_modules/connect-mongo/lib/connect-mongo.js:158:17)
	    at MongoStore.module.exports.MongoStore.get (/path/to/node_modules/connect-mongo/lib/connect-mongo.js:222:10)
	    at Object.session [as middleware] (/path/to/node_modules/express-session/index.js:367:11)
	    at Array.1 (/path/to/build/socketio.js:15:24)
{code}

I opened the application in the debugger and set a breakpoint.  Attached is a screenshot of the debugger in *collection/index.js* around line 84 just before the error occurs.

My installation of MongoDB is from Homebrew.  Here is the version information.

{code:bash}
	$ mongod --version
	db version v2.2.3, pdfile version 4.5
	Sun Jan  4 08:41:22 git version: f570771a5d8a3846eb7586eaffcf4c2f4a96bf08
{code}

According to the *package.json* files, connect-mongo is version 0.4.2 and mongodb is version 1.4.25.

Let me know if you need any more information.

docs: clarify that the timestamp element is for internal use only
description:
Please make sure to clarify in the docs that developers should not be using the 'timestamp' element in their applications. 

Perl's docs are as follows and I think sends the right message:

Perl: "This is an internal type used for replication. It is not for storing dates, times, or timestamps in the traditional sense. Unless you are looking to mess with MongoDB's replication internals, the class you are probably looking for is DateTime. See <MongoDB::DataTypes> for more information." [Clear]

summary: Document that 2.0.x requires specifying replica set name in connection string

In docs/content/tutorials/streams.md:
GridStore = require('mongoddb').GridStore

It should be "mongodb".

summary: Create collection is not failing if the collection already exists
Documents with a key "result" that's an array get clobbered
List collection fails if the the collection name happens to contain the database name
ReplicaSet example missing
description:
The documentation for the 2.0.x driver states "Note that for replicasets the replicaSet query parameter is required in the 2.0 driver" yet there is no example given in either MongoClient.connect() nor the ReplSet documentation.  There needs to be an example someplace if your going to make a change this drastic.

If there is an example of the connection strings, then please link it in the help at least so those of us trying to adopt the latest version stand a chance.

<rant>
The documentation for the 1.4.x driver was amazing giving great examples that actually worked and made sense.

With the release of 2.0.x it seems that the focus is to change the way things work in such a drastic way as to make sure users have to go read the docs.  I'm good with that.  But then make the frigging documentation useful, did you get bought by Oracle?
</rant>

summary: 15 sec db.open delay when one replica set member eth down
description:
We have a three-machine MongoDB replica set. If we turn mongod off on one of them, everything is fine with the rest of the replica set. However, if we suddenly turn off the network interface on one of the machines, all db.open commands take 15 seconds extra. MongoDB works normally from the console at the time. If we drop all mongod traffic with iptables, everything works normally. But turning the interface off will cause the problem.

This causes our load balancer to think all hosts are down (limit is 5 secs) thus blocking all traffic to our HA setup.

summary: Add "How to Ask for Help" section to Github readme
Object prototype causes TypeError: Cannot set property 'auto_reconnect' of undefined
Add .length property to bulk operation objects.
description:
When using bulk op object one needs to be aware of an edge case where no bulk operations are added and .execute method is called - returning error, instead of no op/empty result.

In order to take under account zero ops case one have to keep track of added ops outside of the bulk object and invoke .execute iff there were at least one op added to the object.

In order to simplify this check, it would be convenient if bulk object, for unordered and orderes ops, had .length property.

summary: db.listCollections is not correctly working.
Tailable cursor doesn't work with `$natural: 1` on 2.0.15
Option readPreference is ignored by the driver, but driver check read_preference
mongoDB native node.js driver infinite reconnect attempts
description:
MongoClient.connect returns Db instance, which should be a singleton per node.js process. 
However, if, after getting this Db instance, Mongo server goes down, Collection.toArray method never calls it's callback. The driver simply tries to reconnect infinitely, never calling callback with error. When server comes up again, all toArray calls do call their callbacks successfully, however this is not desirable, the driver should respect re-connection settings.
The code and details are here: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/28400077/mongodb-native-node-js-driver-infinite-reconnect-attempts

summary: setting readPreference via the connection string doesn't work
description: when using a connection string like the following: "mongodb://server1.myserver.com,server2.myserver.com,server3.myserver.com/Stuff?replicaSet=repl1&readPreference=secondary" I would expect the read preference to be secondary. In fact it remains primary. Setting any readPreference this way has no effect

summary: Add Documentation for X.509 external authentication
cursor.count() always returns zero for collections with a dot in the name
description:
The implementation of cursor.count() erroneously assumes that collection names cannot have a dot ('.') character in the name. When it is called against such a collection (e.g. "fs.files") it always returns a count of zero, regardless of the number of documents in the collection.

I have created pull request on Github that includes a failing test and a fix here:

summary: Documentation Seem Broken
description:
The [documentation site|http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/2.0/api/] for the v2.x driver appears to have incorrect labels in the navigation for many of the BSON type functions.

For example the text [Represents a BSON ObjectId type](http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/2.0/api/Represents%2520a%2520BSON%2520ObjectId%2520type..html) is present rather than something like just "ObjectId ( type )"

summary: GridStore.exist does not take ObjectId for filename
description:
If a file is saved with the filename having been set to an ObjectId, the file can be opened at a later date, but the GridStore.exist method does not take this type of parameter into account. Since it only takes a parameter (fileIdObject), it only performs a couple simple checks on this parameter, if it's a string or a RegExp. This doesn't take into account the possibility of an ObjectId for the filename being passed.

I propose changing the parameter from a singular type of just "fileIdObject", which then tries to guess what it is that you are wanting to match, to a more verbose querying object. For example, if the parameter (fileIdObject or whatever) is an actual object, other than a RegExp, that this object is used as the actual querying parameters. If I pass something like { filename: ObjectId("1234567") } that this be used as the query in the collection.findOne call.

summary: Exception in mongodb_scram.js after upgrading server to 3.0.0 without SCRAM-SHA1
description:
After upgrading a sharded cluster to mongodb 3.0.0 without upgrading to SCRAM-SHA1, we've been getting occasional exceptions in our web app every few minutes to an hour or so:

{code}
TypeError: Cannot read property 'done' of null,

summary: toError problem
description:
I have a problem detecting the "Duplicate key" error because is wrapped in an normal Error instance instead of a MongoError.
https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/2.0/lib/utils.js#L107

There is a reason to not use MongoError in this case?

It will be really helpful to handle all these errors in a managed way.

summary: Unordered batch incorrectly tracks batch size when switching batch types
description:
There are two a few issues with in the unordered bulk operation implementation in addToOperationsList: 1) currentBatchSize is updated twice in the function so the batch size will be half of maxWriteBatchSize. 2) currentBatchSize and currentBatchSizeBytes are not reset when switching batches for batch types. As a result it's incorrectly creating a new batch because it thinks it's reached maxWriteBatchSize when it has not. This can result in an empty batch and an "insert must contain at least one document" error. 3) There is some redundant code in the addToOperation function.

I've attached a test script test.js.  There are two variables at the top, insertFirst and batchSize. With insertFirst = false, and batchSize = 1000, the test will fail. If you change batchSize to 999 or insertFirst to true, the test will run without error. This demonstrates issue #2 above. The fix is to track the batch size in the batch instead of in the operation. 

I've attached two files, common.js and unordered.js that fix all problems listed above.

summary: NodeJS driver mishandles poolSize and maxPoolSize options
description:
It appears that the order of options (poolSize, maxPoolSize) being passed via connection string URI changes the behavior of driver connection pool creation.

We have reproduced with the following gist:
https://gist.github.com/chrisckchang/9c3438f04d4b673edd1c
